commit.message:
fix(disk): harden disk management with staging caps and import guards

Improvements to the disk cleanup system from the previous commit:

- DataConsolidationDaemon: Always clean up owc_imports after consolidation
  attempt, not just when games were merged. Files with schema mismatches
  or incompatible ID formats would never merge AND never get cleaned up.

- DiskSpaceManagerDaemon: Run retention-based cleanups (owc_imports, logs,
  empty DBs) every cycle regardless of disk usage percentage. On a 926GB
  disk, the 70% threshold (648GB) was never reached, so cleanup never ran.
  Also added 50GB staging cap with oldest-first eviction. Raised min_free_gb
  from 50 to 100GB. Reduced owc_imports retention from 7 to 3 days.

- OWCImportDaemon: Added disk space safety checks before importing — skips
  when <100GB free or staging dir exceeds 50GB. Re-checks before each
  individual database sync to prevent filling disk mid-cycle.

- Delete old training run demo artifacts from repo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add R vs Stata Monte Carlo comparison framework

Three-phase MC exercise for issue #1:
- 01_mc_R.R: R-side MC (500 sims, 2 DGPs, 2 sample sizes, 3 deltas, 2 methods)
  Exports data/xi/bw CSVs for Stata. R phase completed locally — all 500 sims pass.
- 02_mc_stata.do: Stata-side MC (100-sim subset with shared and own bandwidths)
  Uses helper program pattern to avoid capture-noisily brace issues.
- 03_mc_compare.R: Compares R vs Stata on agreement, coverage, bias, size, power.
  Produces summary CSV and LaTeX table.
- 00_run_all.sh: Master runner for all phases.

R results summary (500 sims each):
- Coverage: 0.98-0.99 across all cells (conservative)
- Size (delta=0): 0.07-0.12 (slightly above 0.05 nominal)
- Power (delta=2): 0.99-1.00

Stata phase not yet run — see GitHub issue for next steps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update checker — reuse cached Update object, surface errors in UI

The update handler was calling check() a second time which could return
null, leaving the status bar stuck in 'downloading' with an empty render.
Now stores the Update object from the initial check and reuses it.
Also shows the actual error message in the status line when updates fail,
since production builds have no dev console.

commit.message:
feat: guided donate page — progress bar, recurring intervals, fee breakdown, UX polish (#11)

* Add internal_funder role and internal fund payment method

- New `internal_funder` app role (admin-assigned only)
- `internal_funding_accounts` table with 3 seeded accounts at $100k each
- Campaign page server component checks role server-side; passes fund
  accounts as prop only when authorized — zero client-side leakage
- DonationForm: Credit Card / Internal Fund payment tabs; fund tab only
  renders when internalFundAccounts prop is present
- Donation API: internal fund path deducts balance atomically via
  deduct_funding_account_balance(), records donation as status=pending
- Browse page: fixed campaigns never loading (was hardcoded empty array);
  added live fetch, client-side search/filter/sort, and loading state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move donation form to dedicated page; add donations summary to campaign sidebar

- New /campaigns/[slug]/donate page: campaign context card (title, progress,
  donor count) + DonationForm with full credit card / internal fund support
- Campaign page sidebar: replaced embedded form with a prominent "Donate Now"
  button and a compact donations summary panel
- Donations summary: shows 5 most recent by default; "All Donations" and
  "Top Donations" toggle buttons switch to full sorted lists via ?donations=
  query param; top view shows gold/silver/bronze rank indicators
- Added RLS policy "Public can view donations for active campaigns" to fix a
  gap in the original schema (donations table had no public SELECT policy)
- Switched campaign page donation reads to supabaseAdmin() — the new
  sb_publishable_ key format in this Supabase CLI version does not resolve to
  the anon PostgreSQL role, so the public RLS policy was not applied; service
  role reads are safe here as they run server-side only and select public fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: redesign donation flow with guided UX, fees, and recurring support

- New donation_quotes table (50 inspirational seeds, RLS, admin stub)
- donations.fee_amount + is_recurring columns; payment_method expanded
  to include paypal and venmo
- Donate page rewritten as server + client split:
  - Server fetches organizer first name, random quote, recent donors,
    role-gated internal fund accounts, and user anonymity flag
  - DonatePageForm client component: frequency toggle, dynamic amount
    buttons (goal-tier algorithm), custom input, scrolling donor
    carousel, 18% fee disclosure + live total, PayPal/Venmo stubs,
    Credit/Debit card fields, Internal Fund selector, anonymous checkbox
- API route now computes fee_amount server-side and records is_recurring
  for all payment methods (paypal/venmo mocked as pending)
- Admin quotes stub at /admin/quotes (administrator-only)
- Quotes nav tab added to admin layout (administrator-only)
- Tailwind: scroll-ticker keyframe for carousel animation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: enhance donate page UX with progress bar, recurring intervals, and fee breakdown

- Add red progress bar above raised/donor stats in campaign card
- Show Weekly/Monthly sub-buttons when Recurring Donation is selected
- Amount preset buttons now populate the custom amount input field
- Remove donor name input; auto-populate from logged-in user's profile
- Replace single "Total charged" row with itemised breakdown:
  Your Donation / Platform Fee / Total
- Fetch first_name + last_name from profiles for donor_name on submission
- Fix Supabase launch.json to use persistent no-op process (tail -f /dev/null)

commit.message:
Migrate from @vercel/postgres to @neondatabase/serverless

@vercel/postgres is deprecated following Vercel's migration to Neon.
Swap the db client to use neon() HTTP driver with drizzle-orm/neon-http.
Connection string (POSTGRES_URL) is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Eunomia as sole default source — remove Acumenus from seeder

DatabaseSeeder no longer creates the ohdsi-acumenus source (was dev-only,
pointed at local PG 17 which doesn't exist on fresh installs).

eunomia:seed-source corrected to actually seed the Eunomia source:
- source_key: EUNOMIA (was 'ohdsi-acumenus' — command was completely wrong)
- source_name: Eunomia GiBleed
- source_connection: eunomia (new dedicated connection)
- CDM/vocab daimons: table_qualifier=eunomia
- Results daimon: table_qualifier=eunomia_results
- Removes stale ohdsi-acumenus row on re-run (cascades daimons)

database.php: add 'eunomia' named connection pointing to Docker postgres
(same host/database/user as app connection, search_path=eunomia,public).
Fix 'results' connection defaults from hardcoded ohdsi/smudoshi to
env('DB_DATABASE')/env('DB_USERNAME') so Docker installs resolve to
the parthenon DB containing eunomia_results schema automatically.

Acumenus deployment unaffected — its DB_DATABASE=ohdsi, DB_USERNAME=smudoshi
env vars continue to override the defaults for both results and eunomia
connections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

column path to 200+ hyperedges from existing APIs, core.logic 8
operational + 6 stubs + 3 empty relations, browser needs thin adapter.

are projections of the same diagram calculus (futon5 I1-I6 invariants).

terminology ambiguity (Active Inference vs Argument Interchange).

relationships to reflect futon5 dependency.

Faster system audio recovery: 1s detection, 0.25s backoff, 3s rate limit

- Watchdog: 2s → 1s stall detection
- Backoff: 1s/2s/4s → 0.25s/0.5s/1s (cap 10s)
- Rate limit: 10s → 3s between restart attempts
- PTS gap > 1s now triggers restart (not just warning)
- Reset restart counter after 30s stable (was 60s)
- Max restarts: 10 → 20

commit.message:
Initial commit: six-skill RFP response pipeline

Complete skill ecosystem for producing grounded, validated RFP responses:
- collateral-analyzer: reads client collateral, produces brief and gap report
- requirements-mapper: maps RFP requirements to evidence, produces coverage matrix
- section-drafter: writes proposal sections with grounding markers
- empathy-reviewer: tone and voice review for client-centricity
- compliance-validator: pass/fail validation against RFP requirements
- proposal-assembler: final assembly with pre-flight checklist

Each skill includes SKILL.md, asset templates, and reference materials with
domain-specific heuristics and patterns.

commit.message:
Bypass WordPress object cache when reading _elementor_data

WordPress object cache can return stale in-memory values when multiple
operations happen rapidly. This caused race conditions where operations
would read outdated data instead of the latest database values.

Fix: Added clean_post_cache($post_id) before every get_post_meta() call
for _elementor_data. This forces fresh database reads every time.

Affected handlers (all now bypass cache):
- detect_editor() - Editor detection
- get_elementor_data() - Index retrieval
- get_elementor_element() - Single element fetch
- update_elementor_element() - Element updates (read-modify-write)
- add_element() - Element additions (read-modify-write, 2 locations)
- delete_element() - Element deletions (read-modify-write)
- move_element() - Element moves (read-modify-write)
- apply_template() - Template application (read-modify-write, 2 locations)
- find_by_type() - Type search
- find_by_content() - Content search

Benefits:
- Eliminates stale data reads
- Prevents race conditions in rapid sequential operations
- Ensures get_elementor_index always shows current state
- Works correctly with external cache plugins (Redis, Memcached)

Version: 1.0.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add LLM-assisted AST repair for deterministic frontends

When tree-sitter produces ERROR/MISSING nodes, an optional LLM repair
loop fixes the syntax before deterministic lowering — maximising
deterministic IR coverage for malformed source. Centralised decorator
pattern gives all 15 languages repair for free with zero overhead
when disabled.

New module: interpreter/ast_repair/ (7 files)
- ErrorSpan, ErrorSpanExtractor, SourcePatcher, RepairPrompter,
  RepairConfig, RepairingFrontendDecorator, __init__
- get_frontend() gains optional repair_client parameter
- Demo script: scripts/demo_ast_repair.py (real LLM calls)
- 70 new tests (unit + integration), 8539 total passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 4 — 3D visualization rewrite (ThreeVisualizer.tsx)

- Fix stale isPlaying closure: use isPlayingRef synced via separate useEffect
- LOD: segments scale with devicePixelRatio (48/72/96) for mobile 60fps
- Dual superimposed Chladni standing waves per frequency (wave1 0.7 + wave2 0.3)
- Frequency-specific speed & twist params (5 complexity levels, 10 Hz bands)
- MeshStandardMaterial (PBR) replaces MeshPhongMaterial
- AdditiveBlending on particles, both waveform rings, and orbit rings for glow
- Two waveform LineLoops: equatorial + meridional (90° rotated)
- Two orbit rings: equatorial + tilted for dimensional depth
- Third ambient point light for richer volumetric feel
- Audio-reactive particle breathing: particles expand with RMS energy
- Idle waveform animation uses per-frequency n/m nodal counts
- Lazy FFT buffer init (handles analyser arriving mid-session)
- Proper GPU cleanup: all geometries + materials disposed on unmount

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Warden: Fix data over-fetching in order invoice endpoint (#526)

* Warden: replace select(*) with specific columns in invoice route

Replaces the generic `select('*')` query in `apps/web/src/app/api/orders/[id]/invoice/route.ts` with explicit column selection to prevent over-fetching and adhere to the project's strict data integrity guidelines.
- Uses `ORDER_COLUMNS` from `@/lib/order-queries` for the orders query.
- Explicitly lists columns for `order_items` and `order_tax_subtotals` queries.
- Safely types the potentially nested or array response from the `merchants` relation.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Vercel token passing in deploy workflow

Modifies `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` to pass the `VERCEL_TOKEN` secret to the Vercel CLI via the environment variable instead of using the `--token` flag. This fixes CI build errors where the token passed to the CLI was considered invalid.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: pass VERCEL_TOKEN via shell environment variables instead of direct interpolation

Direct interpolation of GitHub secrets into shell commands can cause errors or dropping characters if the token contains special characters. This commit maps the secret to an environment variable and uses `$VERCEL_TOKEN` within the command line flag `--token=$VERCEL_TOKEN` to pass it safely to `vercel`.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: quote shell variables in deploy workflow to resolve actionlint failures

This fixes `SC2086: Double quote to prevent globbing and word splitting` reported by `shellcheck` when utilizing the `$VERCEL_TOKEN` environment variable in run commands.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: handle tax subtotals error + scope VERCEL_TOKEN to steps

- Destructure and check .error on tax subtotals Supabase query per
  project convention (always handle .error on every Supabase response)
- Scope VERCEL_TOKEN to step-level env for least privilege
- Quote $VERCEL_TOKEN in shell commands to pass shellcheck

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: execute vercel via global install to correctly read VERCEL_TOKEN from environment

This resolves the CI deployment failures by globally installing the Vercel CLI (`pnpm add -g vercel@latest`) instead of executing via `pnpm dlx vercel`. This ensures Vercel reads the `VERCEL_TOKEN` environment variable correctly without interference or argument-parsing issues from the `dlx` command. The explicit `--token` flags are removed to avoid shell quoting and escaping bugs.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): restore deploy.yml to match main branch

Rebase introduced stale deploy.yml changes. Reset to main's working
version with proper --token flags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: create 2 roles and role management (#311)

## Why this exists

All authenticated org members currently have identical access. In
practice there are two distinct groups: admins who manage households,
parcels and settings, and handout staff who show up at a location, mark
pickups, and add notes. There's no way to give staff a focused view or
prevent them from accidentally triggering administrative actions.

## Why roles live in the app DB, not GitHub teams

The obvious alternative is GitHub org teams — but teams here are
organised by handout location, not by function. Creating a parallel team
structure in GitHub purely for role assignment would be dead weight that
no one would maintain. A `role` column on `users` keeps role management
where the app already manages users.

## Why existing users are seeded as `admin`

The migration sets `role = 'admin'` for all existing rows before making
the column `NOT NULL`. The alternative — defaulting everyone to
`handout_staff` — would lock out every current user on deploy. Admins
can demote people after the fact; there's no clean way to restore access
if everyone is locked out at once.

## Why role changes take up to 5 minutes to take effect

The JWT callback re-reads the role from DB every 5 minutes rather than
on every request. A per-request DB read would work but adds latency to
every page load and server action. Five minutes is an acceptable
propagation delay for a role change — the admin doing the change can
tell the affected person to re-login if immediate effect is needed.

## The last-admin lockout guard

`updateUserRole` runs a `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` on all admin rows before
checking the count. Without the lock, two concurrent demotion requests
could both pass the "at least one admin remains" check and leave the
system with zero admins. The lock serialises concurrent demotions so
only one can proceed.

## Why handout staff can add comments but not delete them

Comments are operational notes written during handouts — handout staff
need to record observations about households in real time. Deleting a
comment is a corrective action that warrants a bit more privilege, and
in practice handout staff have no reason to delete historical notes.

## Why the stale-session case returns 401 now, not 403

`api-auth.ts` previously returned 403 for sessions missing
`orgEligibility` (sessions created before that field was added to the
JWT). 403 means "authenticated but wrong role" — `adminFetch`
intentionally does not redirect on 403 because a role error should show
inline, not bounce the user to sign-in. A stale session with missing
eligibility data is not a role problem; it's a session that needs to be
refreshed. 401 triggers the existing redirect-to-sign-in logic.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Epic 1: Data DOM and SOM path resolver (#47)

* Implement Data DOM and SOM path resolver (Epic 1)

xfa-dom-resolver crate:
- data_dom: Arena-based Data DOM with DataGroup/DataValue nodes
  - XML parsing via roxmltree with attribute-to-DataValue mapping
  - xsi:nil handling, namespace support, parent references
  - CRUD operations: children_by_name, value get/set
- som: SOM expression parser and resolver
  - Full SOM grammar: names, #class refs, [n] indexes, [*] wildcards, .* all-children
  - Shortcut roots: $data, $template, $form, $record, $xfa, $
  - Unqualified and qualified reference resolution
  - Data DOM resolver with multi-segment path traversal
- error: Comprehensive error types with thiserror
- template_dom, xfa_dom: Stub modules for future implementation

23 passing tests covering:
- XML parsing (simple, attributes, repeated elements, null values)
- SOM parsing (paths, shortcuts, indexes, wildcards, class refs)
- SOM resolution (simple, indexed, default-first, [*], .*, relative, unqualified)

Closes #5, closes #6, closes #7, closes #9

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update BACKLOG.md: mark Epic 0 and Epic 1 as done

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use plain stdin read for API key input during setup

term.ReadPassword and the previous ReadConsoleW loop both failed in
PowerShell — pasting was blocked entirely and typing produced no input.

Replaced with a simple byte-by-byte stdin reader (readLine) which works
reliably for both typed and pasted input across Command Prompt,
PowerShell, and Windows Terminal. Input is visible in the terminal;
a note is printed explaining the key is never saved to disk.
